g.title: G
g.text: • Thesis Focus: tlg0093_tlg004.T151115, tlg0656_tlg001.T213927, tlg0007_tlg112.T141553, caley_richards_1956.T212135, katsaros_liritzis_laskaris_2010.T124253

g.title: tlg0656_tlg001.P213927
g.text: {1st Dioscorides recipe} To produce white and
effective lead white in the best variety, as well
as in worse varieties: .Pour most pungent vinegar
into a jar, .Place a piece of lead on a lattice,
.Lay the lattice with the lead onto the mouth of
the jar, .Above, throw over covers, .Wait until
the lead, crumbling down, has strained through the
lattice, .Decant away the clean fluid that floats
on top of the vinegar, .Pour the remaining viscous
fluid into a vessel, .Let the viscous fluid dry
under the sun, .Grind the dried substance, .Sift
the ground substance, .The substance that is
sifted away is the better variety of white and
effective lead white, .Grind and sift what remains
above the sieve of the solid substance {, repeat,
and do it again a third and fourth time}, .The
substances that are sifted away at each repeated
grinding and sifting are progressively worse
varieties of white and effective lead white

g.title: tlg0093_tlg004.T151115
g.text: .Lead the size of a brick is placed over vinegar
in (closed) jars, .They wait for lead to acquire
thickness{, which happens in a maximum of 10
days}, .They open the jars, .They scrape away from
the lead something {resembling a mold}, repeating
this until they have consumed it completely, .They
rub what has been scraped away in a grinding-bowl,
.They always filter off what is being rubbed, .The

g.title: tlg0093_tlg004.q151115004

g.title: tlg0093_tlg004.P151115
g.text: Place a piece of lead over vinegar in closed jars,
.Wait for lead to acquire thickness .Open the
jars, .Scrape away from the lead the substance
that has formed on its surface, repeating this
until you have consumed it completely, .Grind what
you have scraped away, .Filter off what you are
grinding, .The final precipitate is lead white

g.title: tlg0656_tlg001.T213927
g.text: In order for (white and effective) lead white to
come about in the best variety - to be used for
ophthalmological properties - as well as in worse
vinegar into a broad-mouthed jar OR pot-bellied
clay jar, .Place a leaden brick on a lattice of
reeds, .Lay the lattice of reeds with the leaden
brick onto the mouth of the pot, .Above, throw
over covers to prevent vinegar from evaporating,
the clean fluid that floats on top (of the vinegar
after the leaden brick has crumbled down in it),
.Pour the (remaining) viscous fluid into a vessel,
.Let the viscous fluid dry under the sun, .Smooth
the dried substance with a hand-mill OR in any
that is sifted away is the better variety of
(white and effective) lead white, that should be
(with a hand-mill OR in any way) and sift what
remains (above the sieve) of the solid substance
{, repeat, and do it again a third and fourth
time}, .The substances that are sifted away at
each repeated thinning and sifting are

g.title: tlg0007_tlg112.T141553
g.text: When rubbed with vinegar, lead sends forth the
most cooling of deadly drugs, i.e., lead white

g.title: caley_richards_1956.T212135
g.text: We performed in miniature the process described by
Theophrastus to produce lead white: .We supported
blocks of lead just above a 5% acetic acid
solution in an open vessel, .White crusts formed
{at intervals of about 10 days}, we scraped off
these crusts, .We identified the obtained product
as a lead acetate containing only a small
proportion of lead carbonate, .We dissolved this
in tap water, .{Almost immediately} we observed a
turbidity {due to the formation of basic lead
carbonate}, .We let the solution stand for a few
hours, .A considerable amount of lead carbonate
precipitated {due to the solution having absorbed
enough carbon dioxide from the air}, ( .We
obtained lead white in the same way as in

g.title: caley_richards_1956.q163717007
g.text: We let the solution stand for

g.title: katsaros_liritzis_laskaris_2010.T124253
g.text: We reproduced in laboratory the white powder
sample PSI-1 (which is composed of cerussite and
hydrocerussite, and is therefore lead white)
preparation of lead white): .We cut commercial
metallic lead in the shape of a brick {approx. 10
cm length by 5 cm width by 2 cm thickness}, .We
placed vinegar on the bottom of a ceramic vessel,
.We hung the brick-shaped piece of lead over the
vinegar within the ceramic vessel, .We covered the
vessel with a skin {approx. 1 mm (in thickness)}
quasi-transparent to air (i.e., such that it
breathes), .We placed the vessel under the sun,
average 30 °C and a range of 27-55 degrees}, .We
removed the skin cover, .We took the brick-shaped
lead out, .We removed the white powdered layer
(thus formed) on the surface of the lead by
scratching, .In a marble mortar, we washed and
ground down to a fine powder the white powdered
layer using a marble pestle, .We waited for it to
dry out under the sun, ( .We obtained the white
powder sample PSI-1 (which is composed of
cerussite and hydrocerussite, and is therefore
the preparation of lead white) ), .We applied it
on a prepared wooden surface with a gypsum OR
